19	// DefaultFanOutMaxConcurrency caps the number of in-flight per-spoke requests.
20	const DefaultFanOutMaxConcurrency = 8
21	
22	// DefaultFanOutPerNodeTimeout is the default per-node deadline for one fan-out call.
23	const DefaultFanOutPerNodeTimeout = 5 * time.Second
24	
25	// SkipReason is a short, machine-readable code describing why a spoke was
26	// skipped before any HTTP call was issued.
27	type SkipReason string
28	
29	const (
30		// SkipReasonIncompatibleVersion indicates the version handshake against the
31		// hub's own version+schema rejected this spoke (e.g. major mismatch,
32		// schema mismatch, unparseable ddx_version, …). The Handshake reason is
33		// included in the logged message.
34		SkipReasonIncompatibleVersion SkipReason = "incompatible_version"
35		// SkipReasonMissingCapability indicates the spoke does not advertise one of
36		// the capabilities required for this query.
37		SkipReasonMissingCapability SkipReason = "missing_capability"
38		// SkipReasonInvalidURL indicates the spoke's persisted URL did not parse.
39		SkipReasonInvalidURL SkipReason = "invalid_url"
40	)
41	
42	// NodeOutcomeKind describes how a single spoke contributed (or did not
43	// contribute) to a fan-out call. The hub uses this to update per-spoke status
44	// distinctly: offline (immediate transport failure on this fan-out) vs stale
45	// (heartbeat-overdue badge from the registry) vs ok.
46	type NodeOutcomeKind string
47	
48	const (
49		// OutcomeOK means the spoke returned a response (any HTTP 2xx).
50		OutcomeOK NodeOutcomeKind = "ok"
51		// OutcomeOffline means the transport itself failed: connection refused,
52		// DNS error, TLS handshake failed, mid-response disconnect, etc. The hub
53		// flips the spoke's registry status to StatusOffline on this outcome.
54		OutcomeOffline NodeOutcomeKind = "offline"
55		// OutcomeTimeout means the per-node timeout fired before a response was
56		// fully received. Distinct from offline so callers can keep the spoke's
57		// last-known status (likely stale) rather than promote it to offline.
58		OutcomeTimeout NodeOutcomeKind = "timeout"
59		// OutcomeHTTPError means the spoke responded with a non-2xx status. The
60		// transport worked, so we do not flip to offline; the body is captured.
61		OutcomeHTTPError NodeOutcomeKind = "http_error"
62		// OutcomeSkipped means the spoke was filtered out before fan-out; see
63		// NodeResult.SkipReason.
64		OutcomeSkipped NodeOutcomeKind = "skipped"
65	)

67	// FanOutRequest is the GraphQL payload to send to every active spoke.
68	type FanOutRequest struct {
69		Query         string         `json:"query"`
70		OperationName string         `json:"operationName,omitempty"`
71		Variables     map[string]any `json:"variables,omitempty"`
72		// RequiredCapabilities, if non-empty, filters out spokes that do not
73		// advertise every named capability in their SpokeRecord.Capabilities.
74		RequiredCapabilities []string `json:"-"`
75	}

77	// NodeResult is a single spoke's contribution to a fan-out call.
78	type NodeResult struct {
79		NodeID string
80		URL    string
81		// Status, when non-empty, is the registry status the hub should record for
82		// this spoke as a result of this fan-out (StatusOffline on transport
83		// failure). Empty means "do not change registry status".
84		Status SpokeStatus
85		// Outcome classifies how this spoke contributed.
86		Outcome NodeOutcomeKind
87		// Response is the raw GraphQL response body for OutcomeOK. The hub merges
88		// it into the federated response shape. Empty otherwise.
89		Response json.RawMessage
90		// HTTPStatus is the HTTP status code seen, or 0 on transport failure.
91		HTTPStatus int
92		// Err is the underlying error for non-OK outcomes.
93		Err error
94		// SkipReason is set when Outcome == OutcomeSkipped.
95		SkipReason SkipReason
96		// SkipDetail is a free-form human message accompanying SkipReason.
97		SkipDetail string
98		// Duration is the wall time spent on this spoke's call.
99		Duration time.Duration
100	}

102	// FanOutResult is the merged outcome of a fan-out call.
103	type FanOutResult struct {
104		// Responses holds one entry per spoke that returned 2xx, keyed by node_id.
105		// The value is the raw GraphQL response body.
106		Responses map[string]json.RawMessage
107		// Errors holds one entry per spoke that produced any non-OK outcome
108		// (offline, timeout, http_error). Skipped spokes are NOT in this map —
109		// they appear in Skipped instead. Keyed by node_id.
110		Errors map[string]error
111		// Skipped holds one entry per spoke filtered out before fan-out, keyed by
112		// node_id, with the SkipReason as the value.
113		Skipped map[string]SkipReason
114		// StatusUpdates lists the (node_id → new status) changes the hub should
115		// apply to its registry as a side effect of this fan-out. Currently only
116		// transport-level failures (StatusOffline) are emitted here; heartbeat-
117		// driven status (stale) is left to the registry's own time-based reconcile.
118		StatusUpdates map[string]SpokeStatus
119		// Nodes is the per-node detail (every spoke considered, including skipped).
120		// Order matches the input registry order for deterministic test output.
121		Nodes []NodeResult

124	// FanOutClient executes fan-out GraphQL queries across a federation registry.
125	type FanOutClient struct {
126		// HTTPClient is used for spoke /graphql calls. Nil → a default client that
127		// accepts self-signed TLS certs (matches the spoke server's auto-cert).
128		HTTPClient *http.Client

132		PerNodeTimeout time.Duration

143	// NewFanOutClient builds a FanOutClient with defaults filled in.
144	func NewFanOutClient() *FanOutClient {

149				Timeout: 30 * time.Second,

152				},
153			},
154			MaxConcurrency: DefaultFanOutMaxConcurrency,
155			PerNodeTimeout: DefaultFanOutPerNodeTimeout,
156		}

195	// Execute fans out req across spokes. Returns a merged FanOutResult; never
196	// returns a non-nil error for per-spoke failures — those are surfaced through
197	// FanOutResult.Errors. A non-nil error is reserved for misuse (e.g. nil req).
198	//
199	// The set of spokes considered is the input slice. Callers typically pass the
200	// hub registry's spokes filtered by Status == active|stale (offline/degraded
201	// handling depends on policy and is left to the caller). Skipped spokes still
202	// appear in the per-node Nodes detail with OutcomeSkipped + SkipReason.
203	//
204	// Cancellation: if ctx is canceled, in-flight goroutines abort their HTTP
205	// calls and return OutcomeOffline (with ctx.Err() as cause); callers that want
206	// to distinguish caller-cancellation from spoke failure should check ctx.Err()
207	// after Execute returns.
208	func (c *FanOutClient) Execute(ctx context.Context, spokes []SpokeRecord, req *FanOutRequest) (*FanOutResult, error) {
209		if req == nil {
210			return nil, errors.New("federation: fanout request is nil")
211		}
212		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Query) == "" {
213			return nil, errors.New("federation: fanout request query is empty")
214		}
215	
216		body, err := json.Marshal(struct {
217			Query         string         `json:"query"`
218			OperationName string         `json:"operationName,omitempty"`
219			Variables     map[string]any `json:"variables,omitempty"`
220		}{req.Query, req.OperationName, req.Variables})
221		if err != nil {
222			return nil, fmt.Errorf("federation: marshal fanout query: %w", err)
223		}
224	
225		results := make([]NodeResult, len(spokes))
226		sem := make(chan struct{}, c.maxConcurrency())
227		var wg sync.WaitGroup
228	
229		for i := range spokes {
230			i := i
231			s := spokes[i]
232			// Pre-flight filters: version, capability, URL.
233			if skip, reason, detail := c.preflightSkip(s, req.RequiredCapabilities); skip {
234				c.logf("federation fanout: skip node_id=%q reason=%s detail=%q", s.NodeID, reason, detail)
235				results[i] = NodeResult{
236					NodeID:     s.NodeID,
237					URL:        s.URL,
238					Outcome:    OutcomeSkipped,
239					SkipReason: reason,
240					SkipDetail: detail,
241				}
242				continue
243			}
244	
245			wg.Add(1)
246			go func() {
247				defer wg.Done()
248				select {
249				case sem <- struct{}{}:
250				case <-ctx.Done():
251					results[i] = NodeResult{
252						NodeID:  s.NodeID,
253						URL:     s.URL,
254						Outcome: OutcomeOffline,
255						Status:  StatusOffline,
256						Err:     ctx.Err(),
257					}
258					return
259				}
260				defer func() { <-sem }()
261				results[i] = c.callOne(ctx, s, body)
262			}()
263		}
264		wg.Wait()
265	
266		out := &FanOutResult{
267			Responses:     map[string]json.RawMessage{},
268			Errors:        map[string]error{},
269			Skipped:       map[string]SkipReason{},
270			StatusUpdates: map[string]SpokeStatus{},
271			Nodes:         results,
272		}
273		for _, r := range results {
274			switch r.Outcome {
275			case OutcomeOK:
276				out.Responses[r.NodeID] = r.Response
277			case OutcomeSkipped:
278				out.Skipped[r.NodeID] = r.SkipReason
279			default:
280				if r.Err != nil {
281					out.Errors[r.NodeID] = r.Err
282				} else {
283					out.Errors[r.NodeID] = fmt.Errorf("fanout: %s", r.Outcome)
284				}
285			}
286			if r.Status != "" {
287				out.StatusUpdates[r.NodeID] = r.Status
288			}
289		}
290		return out, nil
291	}
292	
293	// preflightSkip returns (true, reason, detail) when the spoke must be skipped
294	// before any HTTP call is issued.
295	func (c *FanOutClient) preflightSkip(s SpokeRecord, requiredCaps []string) (bool, SkipReason, string) {
296		if c.HubDDxVersion != "" && c.HubSchemaVersion != "" {
297			hs := Handshake(c.HubDDxVersion, c.HubSchemaVersion, s.DDxVersion, s.SchemaVersion)
298			if !hs.Accept {
299				return true, SkipReasonIncompatibleVersion, hs.Reason
300			}
301		}
302		if len(requiredCaps) > 0 {
303			have := map[string]struct{}{}
304			for _, c := range s.Capabilities {
305				// Skip the synthetic identity-fingerprint capability.
306				if strings.HasPrefix(c, "@identity:") {
307					continue
308				}
309				have[c] = struct{}{}
310			}
311			for _, want := range requiredCaps {
312				if _, ok := have[want]; !ok {
313					return true, SkipReasonMissingCapability, want
314				}
315			}
316		}
317		if strings.TrimSpace(s.URL) == "" {
318			return true, SkipReasonInvalidURL, "empty url"
319		}
320		if _, err := url.Parse(s.URL); err != nil {
321			return true, SkipReasonInvalidURL, err.Error()
322		}
323		return false, "", ""
324	}
325	
326	// callOne issues the GraphQL POST against one spoke under per-node timeout.
327	func (c *FanOutClient) callOne(ctx context.Context, s SpokeRecord, body []byte) NodeResult {
328		start := c.now()
329		cctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, c.perNodeTimeout())
330		defer cancel()
331	
332		endpoint := strings.TrimRight(s.URL, "/") + "/graphql"
333		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(cctx, http.MethodPost, endpoint, bytes.NewReader(body))
334		if err != nil {
335			return NodeResult{
336				NodeID:   s.NodeID,
337				URL:      s.URL,
338				Outcome:  OutcomeOffline,
339				Status:   StatusOffline,
340				Err:      err,
341				Duration: c.now().Sub(start),
342			}
343		}
344		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
345		req.Header.Set("Accept", "application/json")
346	
347		resp, err := c.httpClient().Do(req)
348		if err != nil {
349			// Distinguish per-node timeout (deadline exceeded on cctx but parent
350			// ctx still live) from caller cancellation and from generic transport
351			// failure. Per-node timeout → keep last-known status; transport
352			// failure → flip to offline.
353			if cctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded && ctx.Err() == nil {
354				return NodeResult{
355					NodeID:   s.NodeID,
356					URL:      s.URL,
357					Outcome:  OutcomeTimeout,
358					Err:      fmt.Errorf("per-node timeout after %s: %w", c.perNodeTimeout(), err),
359					Duration: c.now().Sub(start),
360				}
361			}
362			return NodeResult{
363				NodeID:   s.NodeID,
364				URL:      s.URL,
365				Outcome:  OutcomeOffline,
366				Status:   StatusOffline,
367				Err:      err,
368				Duration: c.now().Sub(start),
369			}
370		}
371		defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()
372	
373		buf, readErr := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
374		if readErr != nil {
375			// Mid-response disconnect — transport-level failure → offline.
376			return NodeResult{
377				NodeID:     s.NodeID,
378				URL:        s.URL,
379				Outcome:    OutcomeOffline,
380				Status:     StatusOffline,
381				HTTPStatus: resp.StatusCode,
382				Err:        fmt.Errorf("read body: %w", readErr),
383				Duration:   c.now().Sub(start),
384			}
385		}
386	
387		if resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300 {
388			snippet := string(buf)
389			if len(snippet) > 256 {
390				snippet = snippet[:256] + "…"
391			}
392			return NodeResult{
393				NodeID:     s.NodeID,
394				URL:        s.URL,
395				Outcome:    OutcomeHTTPError,
396				HTTPStatus: resp.StatusCode,
397				Err:        fmt.Errorf("spoke returned HTTP %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, snippet),
398				Duration:   c.now().Sub(start),
399			}
400		}
401	
402		return NodeResult{
403			NodeID:     s.NodeID,
404			URL:        s.URL,
405			Outcome:    OutcomeOK,
406			HTTPStatus: resp.StatusCode,
407			Response:   json.RawMessage(buf),
408			Duration:   c.now().Sub(start),
409		}
410	}

Commit landed cleanly with all hooks green.

Summary: Added federated GraphQL surface (federationNodes, federatedBeads, federatedRuns, federatedProjects) with routing metadata (nodeId, projectId, projectUrl, writeCapability, status) on each row. Resolvers wire to B14.6a fan-out via a FederationProvider interface; existing local query types are unchanged (parallel ship). Integration tests against two mock spoke servers verify shape, partial-result handling, and version-skew rendering.
